stem: Find the area of the region \(D\) enclosed by the parabola \( y = [[a]]x^2 \) and the line \( y = [[m]]x \), by evaluating \( \iint_D 1\,dA \).
solution: The curves meet where \([[a]]x^2 = [[m]]x\), i.e. at \(x=0\) and \(x=[[m]]/[[a]]\), with the line above the parabola. Thus the area is \( \int_{0}^{[[m]]/[[a]]}\int_{[[a]]x^2}^{[[m]]x} 1\,dy\,dx = \int_{0}^{[[m]]/[[a]]} ([[m]]x - [[a]]x^2)\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This integrates only under the line (down to \(y=0\)), forgetting to subtract the area under the parabola.
This keeps only the parabola contribution \(\int a x^2\,dx\) with the wrong overall structure.
This computes parabola minus line, giving the negative of the true area.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the area of the region \(D\) enclosed by the curve \( x = [[a]]y^2 \) and the line \( x = [[m]]y \), by setting up \( \iint_D 1\,dA \) as a Type II integral (integrating in \(x\) first).
solution: Treat \(D\) as a Type II region: for each \(y\) between \(0\) and \([[m]]/[[a]]\), \(x\) runs from the curve \(x=[[a]]y^2\) to the line \(x=[[m]]y\). So the area is \( \int_{0}^{[[m]]/[[a]]}\int_{[[a]]y^2}^{[[m]]y} 1\,dx\,dy = \int_{0}^{[[m]]/[[a]]} ([[m]]y - [[a]]y^2)\,dy = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This integrates only out to the line (down to \(x=0\)), forgetting to subtract the region under the curve.
This keeps only the curve's contribution \(\int a y^2\,dy\) with the wrong overall structure.
This computes curve minus line, giving the negative of the true area.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \(D\) be the region bounded by \( y = 0 \), \( y = [[a]]x^2 + [[c]] \), \( x = [[p]] \), and \( x = [[q]] \). Find the area of \(D\) by evaluating \( \iint_D 1\,dA \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the wrong power \(x^3/2\) instead of \(x^3/3\) for the antiderivative of \(x^2\).
This uses \(x^2/3\)-style bookkeeping and drops a factor from the correct antiderivative.
This forgets to divide by 3 when antidifferentiating \([[a]]x^2\).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the area of the region \(D\) enclosed between the upward-opening parabola \( y = [[a]]x^2 + [[c1]] \) and the downward-opening parabola \( y = [[c2]] - [[b]]x^2 \), by evaluating \( \iint_D 1\,dA \).
solution: The two parabolas meet where \([[a]]x^2+[[c1]] = [[c2]]-[[b]]x^2\), i.e. \( ([[a]]+[[b]])x^2 = [[cgap]] \), giving \(x = \pm[[k]]\). So the area is \( \int_{-[[k]]}^{[[k]]} \left(([[c2]]-[[b]]x^2) - ([[a]]x^2+[[c1]])\right)dx = \int_{-[[k]]}^{[[k]]} \left([[cgap]] - ([[a]]+[[b]])x^2\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the cubic term entirely and just uses \(2\cdot[[cgap]]\cdot[[k]]\) doubled incorrectly.
This doubles the correct antiderivative coefficient by mistake.
This drops the leading \(2\cdot[[cgap]]\cdot[[k]]\) term, keeping only the cubic correction.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
